page: Microsoft Azure E8s_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the ESv4 server family. It runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and is powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor using the x86_64 architecture. The configuration features 8 dedicated vCPUs derived from 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled operating at a base frequency of 2.6 GHz. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable for remote disk attachments.

Performance benchmarks place the E8s_v4 in the average tier for most compute and memory operations. The single-core and multi-core stress-ng tests, alongside Geekbench scores, demonstrate stable mid-range CPU capabilities. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and cryptography standards like AES. However, memory bandwidth tests reveal a performance tradeoff: while cached small-block reads achieve a high throughput of 403.65 GB/sec, uncached large-block reads and writes drop to a weaker bottom-25% tier at 29.42 GB/sec and 24.3 GB/sec respectively. Similarly, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and medium-sized LLM inference tasks show lower relative performance.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E8s_v4 offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio without the added cost of bundled local SSDs or GPU hardware, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high-speed local scratch space. It is well-suited for relational database operations, caching layers such as Redis, static web serving, and general-purpose enterprise applications requiring a larger memory footprint.
description: Microsoft Azure E8s_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the ESv4 family, running on Microsoft Hyper-V. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL x86_64 processor, it provides 8 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores at 2.6 GHz, paired with 64.0 GB of RAM. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Benchmark data indicates average performance for standard compute, database operations, and cached memory reads, though uncached large-block memory bandwidth is relatively weak. This hardware profile is best suited for memory-intensive workloads like database hosting, Redis caching, and enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E8s_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB of RAM, and Premium Storage capability, designed for database and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E8s_v4 is a memory-optimized VM with 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 64 GB of RAM, ideal for database and caching workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 virtual machine offering a high memory-to-core ratio for database hosting and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor
64.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU
Premium Storage capable with no bundled local SSD storage
Average performance in database operations and Redis caching
Optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads and caching layers
